creative_brief: Hero image: Rachel on her Kempsey farm at golden hour, looking directly into camera with quiet conviction — not smiling for the lens, but present. Below the fold, intercut close-up product shots (Bundarra salami, Paris Creek dairy, Collins seafood on ice) with candid farmer portraits. Earthy palette, no gloss. Typography in sentence case, generous whitespace. Feels like a letter, not a billboard.
headline: One afternoon in Kempsey changed how I feed my family
subhead: I stood in a farmhouse kitchen watching Rachel Ward pull vegetables from soil I could touch. Then I thought about the plastic-wrapped tray in my fridge at home — and couldn't remember a single thing about where it came from.
hero_copy: I'd spent years trusting labels. "Farm fresh." "Locally sourced." "Natural." I never asked fresh since when, sourced from where, natural according to whom. That afternoon I realised I'd been feeding my kids a supply chain, not food. The farmer couldn't tell you my name. The supermarket couldn't tell me theirs. Nobody in the middle cared, because nobody in the middle had to. I started FarmThru to close that gap — not with a slogan, but with a grocery store where every product traces back to a real farm, a real person, and a practice that gives back to the land.

sections.heading: The gap between what we're told and what we're sold
sections.body: Supermarket supply chains are built for efficiency, not transparency. Your beef might travel thousands of kilometres before it hits the shelf. Your dairy passes through wholesalers, warehouses, and weeks of cold storage. By the time you buy it, the farmer who raised it has already been paid a fraction of what you spent. FarmThru works differently. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Zero middlemen. We connect you directly to farms like Paris Creek in South Australia — carbon-neutral, biodynamic dairy — and Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast of NSW, bringing seasonal Australian seafood and wild-caught Alaskan salmon straight from the source. You order online. You collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. That's it. No subscription. No lock-in. Just groceries from farms we'll actually name.

sections.heading: I only stock what I'd feed my own kids
sections.body: That's not a tagline — it's the filter. Every supplier on FarmThru meets it. Bundarra Farm's pork belly and fennel salami. Farmer Brown's eggs from NSW. Little Yarran Farm. Rachel Ward's regenerative operation in Kempsey. I've visited these farms. I've met the families running them. I know how they raise their animals and what they put in their soil. When you shop with FarmThru, you'll know too. We'll show you where your food comes from — because that question shouldn't feel radical.

sections.heading: This isn't just a store — and it doesn't have to be just mine
sections.body: FarmThru is building something the big chains will never offer: a grocery model where farmers are paid fairly, food is fresher, and regenerative practice is the standard — not the premium. We're opening our equity crowdfunding campaign so you can own a piece of it. Not as a customer. As a partner. This is early. There's real risk — like any business at this stage, there are no guaranteed returns, and you could lose what you invest. But if you believe the food system needs more than another label, you can be part of building the alternative. Join the waitlist. When the campaign opens, you'll be first to see the details and decide for yourself.

sections.heading: No commitment, no pressure — just a seat at the table
sections.body: Place a $5 refundable deposit to join the VIP waitlist. You'll get early access to the crowdfunding campaign before it goes public. No obligation to invest. No obligation to buy. And when the store launches, order when you want — collect from Brookvale, Monday to Friday. That's the whole model. Simple enough that a farmer could explain it. Transparent enough that you'd want them to.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is conducted under the Australian crowd-sourced funding regime. Any investment involves risk, including the risk of losing your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are guaranteed. This page is not financial advice. You should read the offer document and consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances before making any decision.
